TypeSignature.Value: .class public sequential ansi sealed beforefieldinit HBColor extends System.ValueType implements class System.IEquatable`1<valuetype HarfBuzzSharp.HBColor>

Docs.summary: Represents a color with red, green, blue, and alpha components, stored as a packed 32-bit unsigned integer.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
`HBColor` is a value type that stores a color as a packed 32-bit unsigned integer using the byte order `0xBBGGRRAA`, where the blue component occupies the most-significant byte and the alpha component occupies the least-significant byte. This layout differs from <xref:SkiaSharp.SKColor>, which uses `0xAARRGGBB`.
Use the extension methods in <xref:SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz.ColorExtensions> to convert between `HBColor` and SkiaSharp color types.
## Examples
```csharp var color = new HBColor(255, 128, 0, 255); // red=255, green=128, blue=0, alpha=255 Console.WriteLine($"R={color.Red} G={color.Green} B={color.Blue} A={color.Alpha}"); ```

MemberSignature.Value: public HBColor (uint value);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int32 value) cil managed

Docs.param: The packed color value in the format 0xBBGGRRAA.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of from a packed 32-bit value.

.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int8 red, unsigned int8 green, unsigned int8 blue, unsigned int8 alpha) cil managed

Docs.param: The alpha (opacity) component (0–255), where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of with the specified color components.
